title: Understanding the Pythagorean Theorem
text: The Pythagorean Theorem is a fundamental relation in Euclidean geometry among the three sides of a right triangle. It states that the square of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle) is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two sides.

title: Identify the Known Values
text: In this problem, you are given one leg of the triangle as $$7$$ cm and the hypotenuse as $$25$$ cm. You need to find the length of the other leg.

title: Set Up the Equation
text: Using the Pythagorean Theorem formula $$a^2+b^2=c^2$$, substitute the known values: $$7^2+b^2={25}^2$$.

title: Calculate the Squares
text: Calculate the squares of the known values: $$7^2$$ and $${25}^2$$.

title: Solve for the Unknown
text: Rearrange the equation to solve for $$b^2$$: $$b^2={25}^2-7^2$$.

title: Find the Length of the Other Leg
text: Take the square root of both sides to find b: $$b=\sqrt{576}$$.
